sourceMovie.title: 10 Things I Hate About You
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit 10 Things I Hate About You for the same reason it broke through in 1999: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Gil Junger keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: High School Musical
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 10 Things I Hate About You clicked for you, High School Musical (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: High School Musical 3: Senior Year
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 10 Things I Hate About You clicked for you, High School Musical 3: Senior Year (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after 10 Things I Hate About You?
faq.answer: Try High School Musical, High School Musical 3: Senior Year, Sierra Burgess Is a Loser—each shares DNA with 10 Things I Hate About You while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is 10 Things I Hate About You?
faq.answer: 10 Things I Hate About You (1999) is best described as Comedy, Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is 10 Things I Hate About You part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "10 Things I Hate About... Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
